candidates.evidence_quote: Revenues increased 4.7% to $3,751.8 million for fiscal 2025 compared to $3,581.7 million for fiscal 2024, primarily due to additional revenue related to new restaurant openings.
Our revenue growth is primarily driven by new restaurant openings and increases in comparable restaurant sales.
North Italia comparable sales decreased approximately 2% from fiscal 2024.
Flower Child comparable sales increased approximately 5% from fiscal 2024.
partially offset by decreased customer traffic of 2.3%
The increase from fiscal 2024 was primarily driven by an increase in average check of 2.4%
We implemented menu price increases of approximately 2.4% and 1.5% in the first and third quarters of fiscal 2025, respectively.
partially offset by a 1.9% negative change from menu mix
Sales through the off-premise channel comprised approximately 21% of our restaurant sales during both fiscal 2025 and fiscal 2024.
due primarily to favorable commodity inflation across most categories (0.5%) and a shift in sales mix (0.2%)
As a percentage of revenues, food and beverage costs were 21.7% for fiscal 2025 compared to 22.5% for fiscal 2024
labor expenses, which include restaurant-level labor costs and bakery production labor, including associated fringe benefits, were 35.0% and 35.3% in fiscal 2025 and fiscal 2024, respectively
This decrease was primarily due to menu price increases in excess of wage rate inflation and improved staffing levels (0.5%)
This decrease was primarily due to menu price increases in excess of wage rate inflation and improved staffing levels (0.5%)
partially offset by higher group medical cost due to larger claim activity (0.3%)
As a result, in fiscal 2025, we recognized $17.3 million of additional gift card breakage.
This variance was primarily driven by higher facility-related costs (0.2%).
As a percentage of revenues, G&A expenses were 6.5% and 6.4% for fiscal 2025 and fiscal 2024, respectively.
During fiscal 2025, we recorded $23.0 million of expense primarily related to the impairment of long-lived assets
lease termination expense, net related to two Grand Lux Cafes (one that closed in fiscal 2023 and one that closed in early 2026) and one Other FRC (that closed in early 2026)

In fiscal 2025, we recorded a $10.5 million increase in the fair value of the contingent consideration and compensation liability primarily stemming from updating the probability of achievement due to passage of time as well as recent performance
Preopening costs were $33.1 million for fiscal 2025 compared to $27.5 million for fiscal 2024.
In fiscal 2025, we recorded a $15.9 million loss on early debt extinguishment.
In fiscal 2025, we had an income tax provision of $14.5 million, an effective tax rate of 8.9%, compared to an income tax provision of $14.3 million, an effective tax rate of 8.3%, in fiscal 2024.
Cash flows from operations increased by $33.0 million from fiscal 2024 primarily due to higher net income after excluding non-cash activity
We anticipate approximately $210 million in capital expenditures to support this level of unit development, as well as required maintenance on our restaurants.
We expect to open as many as 26 new restaurants in fiscal 2026 across our portfolio of concepts, with approximately one third of the openings occurring in the first half of fiscal 2026.
On February 28, 2025, we issued $575.0 million in aggregate principal amount of convertible senior notes
In the first quarter of fiscal 2025, we repaid $110.0 million on the Revolver Facility.

Under the Revolver Facility, we are subject to financial covenants, as well as to customary events of default that, if triggered, could result in acceleration of the maturity of the Revolver Facility.
In total, we repurchased 2.9 million shares at a cost of $153.9 million, excluding excise tax, during fiscal 2025.
Common stock dividends of $52.2 million and $53.0 million were paid in fiscal 2025 and 2024, respectively.
Similar to many restaurant and retail chain store operations, we utilize operating lease arrangements for all of our restaurant locations.
Our objective is to drive margin expansion over time by leveraging incremental sales to increase restaurant-level margins at The Cheesecake Factory concept
Total operating weeks at North Italia increased 16.5% to 2,355 in fiscal 2025 compared to 2,021 in the prior year.
North Italia average sales per restaurant operating week decreased 0.9% to $146,877 in fiscal 2025 from $148,231 in fiscal 2024.
partnering with a third party to provide delivery services for our restaurants
continuing to offer innovative, high quality menu items that offer customers a wide range of options in terms of flavor, price and value
leveraging our bakery operations, international and consumer packaged goods royalty revenue streams
Our bakery division operates two facilities that produce quality cheesecakes and other baked products for our restaurants, international licensees and third-party bakery customers.
augmenting our marketing programs, including our Cheesecake Rewards ® program

notes: Chunk contains 1 event: CAKE 10-K accession 0001104659-26-018643 (2026-02-23), part 5 of 6 (MD&A: results of operations, non-GAAP reconciliations, liquidity and capital resources); no fiscal_kpis in this chunk.
Names coined only from this part; other parts of the same 10-K are handled by other bots and re-unioned later.

Skipped 9 items: brand-level Cheesecake Factory comparable sales (brand string = company legal name, banned in name; folded into company-wide same_store_sales evidence); Other FRC sales (FRC = subsidiary acronym/legal name, banned); shift in sales mix in food costs (vague, overlaps menu_mix); gift card inventory write-down (one-off state-word event); uncertain tax positions reserve and employment credits (covered by effective_tax_rate); interest expense (table-only, no narrative attribution); stock-price-vs-guidance risk-factor cross-reference (effect on stock, not a cause); conversion-rate adjustment to convertible notes (single-instance mechanics).

Banned-word handling: 'new restaurant openings'/'unit growth' evidence named as restaurant_development (opening/growth are banned tokens); forward fiscal-2026 unit outlook named restaurant_development_guidance per {metric}_guidance convention.
wage_inflation and restaurant_staffing share one evidence sentence that names two independent causal variables (R2 split).
